---
applyTo: "content/users/**/*.md,content/users/**/profile.yaml"
---

Review content pull requests for factual quality and safety first.

- Treat unsupported or contradictory claims as blocking issues. Ask for correction or sources instead of guessing.
- Reject AI-authored content, spam, fake marketing, affiliate bait, political campaigning, malicious instructions, phishing, credential theft, malware, or other harmful behavior.
- If notes are added under `content/users/<id>/`, make sure that user also has `content/users/<id>/profile.yaml`.
- If a note is too thin, placeholder-like, or missing obvious sourcing for factual claims, remind the contributor before approval.
- If the PR is purely knowledge sharing, policy-safe, factually sound, and contribution requirements are satisfied, approve it.
- Keep comments short, concrete, and tied to exact file paths.
